message:
refactor(test): decompose cross-entity liveness contract test into focused assertions

The monolithic cross_entity_liveness_contract_composes_theorem_chain_
into_harness_consumers test was 230+ lines of sequential assertions
touching identity, invariant links, theorem chains, harness links, and
end-to-end guarantees — making it difficult to identify which contract
aspect failed and why.

Extracted five focused assertion functions:
- assert_cross_entity_contract_identity: contract_id + source_bead
- assert_cross_entity_invariant_links: invariant_id coverage + link_map
- assert_cross_entity_theorem_chain: theorem ordering + segment coverage
- assert_cross_entity_harness_links: harness field mapping + verification
- assert_cross_entity_end_to_end_guarantees: guarantee theorem/harness refs
- cross_entity_harness_field_map: helper to build harness→fields BTreeMap

The top-level test now reads as a clear pipeline: parse JSON → check
identity → check invariants → build theorem index → check theorem chain
→ build harness map → check harness links → check guarantees.

No behavioral changes; identical coverage, just better failure locality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
